title: Answer submission
description: The minimal structured output every implementation returns for a catalog question (ADR 0005, DESIGN.md §6.4): a natural-language answer + key numeric value(s) + optional provenance. `behavior` lets the adversarial tier (ADR 0013) encode expected behavior -- and a submission report its actual behavior -- so ambiguous/trap questions grade as objectively as straight ones. This schema governs both submitted answers and the behavior-encoding of gold answers.

question_id.description: The catalog question id (spec/questions/catalog.yaml). Ties the submission to its gold answer.

answer.description: Natural-language answer. For clarification/refusal behaviors, the question asked or the reason to refuse.

key_values.description: The key numeric value(s) graded against gold. Shape is per-question (e.g. flagged rows for surveillance); may be empty {} when behavior is a clarification request or a refusal.

behavior.description: What the implementation did. Straight questions expect `answered`; the adversarial tier gold-encodes the expected value here.

provenance.description: Optional: the governed metrics/dimensions/filters/entities used to produce the answer.
